description:
You are assisting a capacity–planning engineer who keeps monthly raw-usage logs under 

  /home/user/capacity/raw/

and publishes a single averaged “summary” file for each month under  

  /home/user/capacity/reports/

Your job is to automate the January-2023 publication.

1. Averages  
   • Read every file whose name ends with “_202301.log” inside /home/user/capacity/raw/.  
   • Each raw file already contains exactly three lines, in the fixed order shown below:  
        CPU_USED=<integer 0-100>  
        MEM_USED=<integer 0-100>  
        DISK_USED=<integer 0-100>  
   • Compute the arithmetic mean of the value for each metric across all January files, rounding **down** to the nearest integer.  
   • Create /home/user/capacity/reports/summary_202301.log containing **exactly** the three lines, in this order and format:  
        AVG_CPU_USED=<integer>  
        AVG_MEM_USED=<integer>  
        AVG_DISK_USED=<integer>

2. Symlink management  
   • Ensure /home/user/capacity/latest is a symbolic link that points to the new summary_202301.log.  
   • If /home/user/capacity/latest already exists (either as a file or a symlink), replace it so that it **unambiguously** resolves to the freshly-created summary file.  
   • The link target must be the absolute path /home/user/capacity/reports/summary_202301.log (not a relative path).

3. Audit log  
   • Append an entry to /home/user/capacity/operations.log **as a single line** whose exact format is:  
        2023-01 publication completed → <UTC-timestamp>  
     where <UTC-timestamp> is produced by the command  date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'  
   • Do not add any other text or extra blank lines to operations.log.

Verification notes (strict):  
• Path names are case-sensitive.  
• summary_202301.log must have only the three required lines with no trailing blank line.  
• /home/user/capacity/latest must be a symbolic link (the test harness will use stat -c %F).  
• The timestamp in operations.log must be in UTC and ISO-8601 “Z” form.  
• File permissions should remain default user-writable; no root privileges are available.

You may perform the steps in any order, combine commands with “&&” or “;”, or run them in parallel background processes, but all three requirements must be satisfied before you finish.
truth:
Existing directory tree before the agent starts:

/home/user/capacity/
/home/user/capacity/raw/
/home/user/capacity/reports/

Files already present in /home/user/capacity/raw/ :

1. /home/user/capacity/raw/server1_202301.log  
   Content (exact, no blank lines):
CPU_USED=42
MEM_USED=76
DISK_USED=63

2. /home/user/capacity/raw/server2_202301.log  
   Content (exact, no blank lines):
CPU_USED=58
MEM_USED=68
DISK_USED=57

No other “_202301.log” files exist.

Expected math:
AVG_CPU_USED = floor((42 + 58) / 2) = 50  
AVG_MEM_USED = floor((76 + 68) / 2) = 72  
AVG_DISK_USED = floor((63 + 57) / 2) = 60  

Therefore the correct /home/user/capacity/reports/summary_202301.log must be exactly:

AVG_CPU_USED=50
AVG_MEM_USED=72
AVG_DISK_USED=60

There must be **no** newline after the final 60.

Symlink:
/home/user/capacity/latest → /home/user/capacity/reports/summary_202301.log  
(stat -c %N should show the arrow to that absolute path)

Audit log:
/home/user/capacity/operations.log should end with a single line matching the regex:  
^2023-01 publication completed → 20[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$

operations.log may contain earlier lines; the test will only check that the final line matches.

Permissions: All files/directories are owned by user:user, mode 755 for directories, 644 for files.
